problem.title: Flip Game
problem.description: You are playing the following Flip Game with your friend: Given a string that contains only these two characters: + and -, you and your friend take turns to flip two consecutive '++' into '--'.
assumptions: A move flips exactly one '++' into '--'.

example.explanation: Replace each '++' pair with '--'. Return all states after one move.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – String Manipulation
solutions.description: Scan the string for every pair of consecutive '+' characters and generate a new state by replacing that pair with '--'. Each valid replacement represents one possible move the current player can make. The key insight is that only '+'+' pairs are legal moves, so a single linear scan is sufficient to enumerate all possibilities.
